title: A Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits
relevance: Introduces residual-stream path expansion and QK/OV composition for reverse-engineering transformer circuits.

title: In-context Learning and Induction Heads
relevance: Connects induction-head formation to in-context learning using correlational and causal evidence across training.

title: Softmax Linear Units
relevance: Tests an architectural intervention intended to make individual language-model neurons easier to interpret.

title: Toy Models of Superposition
authors: Nelson Elhage; Tristan Hume; Catherine Olsson; Nicholas Schiefer et al.
relevance: Provides the canonical toy-model account of feature superposition and its geometry and phase changes.

title: Superposition, Memorization, and Double Descent
relevance: Relates feature superposition and memorization to double-descent dynamics in controlled toy models.

title: Privileged Bases in the Transformer Residual Stream
relevance: Shows that residual-stream coordinates can become unexpectedly privileged and complicate basis-invariant accounts.

title: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning
authors: Trenton Bricken; Adly Templeton et al.
relevance: Establishes sparse autoencoders as a practical feature-decomposition method on a one-layer transformer.

title: Scaling Monosemanticity: Extracting Interpretable Features from Claude 3 Sonnet
authors: Adly Templeton; Tom Conerly et al.
relevance: Demonstrates frontier-scale SAE feature extraction while leaving the Claude model and learned dictionaries closed.

title: Using Dictionary Learning Features as Classifiers

title: Sparse Crosscoders for Cross-Layer Features and Model Diffing
relevance: Introduces joint dictionaries for persistent cross-layer features and shared or model-exclusive representations.

title: Evaluating Feature Steering: A Case Study in Mitigating Social Biases
relevance: Measures useful steering ranges and off-target effects and compares feature steering with prompting.

title: Stage-Wise Model Diffing
authors: Trenton Bricken; Siddharth Mishra-Sharma et al.
relevance: Tracks representational changes across training stages and illustrates high-sensitivity but low-specificity model diffing.

title: Insights on Crosscoder Model Diffing
relevance: Diagnoses dense and polysemantic exclusive dictionaries caused by capacity competition and proposes a shared-feature adjustment.

title: Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language Models
authors: Emmanuel Ameisen; Jack Lindsey; Adam Pearce; Wes Gurnee et al.
relevance: Introduces cross-layer-transcoder attribution graphs and interventions for tracing prompt-specific computations.

title: On the Biology of a Large Language Model
relevance: Applies attribution graphs to ten Claude behaviors and documents both mechanistic case studies and limited coverage.

title: Progress on Attention
authors: Adam Jermyn; Jack Lindsey et al.
relevance: Reports evidence for attention superposition and outlines unresolved QK and cross-layer attention problems.

title: Sparse Mixtures of Linear Transforms

title: Persona Vectors: Monitoring and Controlling Character Traits in Language Models
authors: Runjin Chen; Andy Arditi; Henry Sleight; Owain Evans; Jack Lindsey
relevance: Uses mean-difference activation directions for trait monitoring steering and preventative training.

title: Tracing Attention Computation Through Feature Interactions
authors: Harish Kamath; Emmanuel Ameisen et al.
relevance: Decomposes QK attention scores into bilinear feature-pair contributions and proposes their integration into attribution graphs.

title: A Toy Model of Mechanistic (Un)Faithfulness

title: When Models Manipulate Manifolds: The Geometry of a Counting Task
relevance: Combines curved feature manifolds and QK/OV analysis in a causally tested line-counting circuit.

title: Emergent Introspective Awareness in Large Language Models
relevance: Uses concept-injection interventions to test limited introspective access while documenting confabulation and low success rates.

title: Activation Oracles
authors: Adam Karvonen; James Chua et al.

title: The Assistant Axis: Situating and Stabilizing the Character of Large Language Models

relevance: Identifies an assistant-persona direction and tests drift monitoring and activation capping across open models.

title: Cross-Architecture Model Diffing with Crosscoders
relevance: Partitions shared and model-exclusive features across unrelated open-model architectures as a high-recall difference finder.

title: Mechanisms of Introspective Awareness
authors: Uzay Macar; Li Yang; Atticus Wang; Peter Wallich; Emmanuel Ameisen; Jack Lindsey
relevance: Localizes evidence-carrier and gating mechanisms for introspection in open Gemma and OLMo models.

title: Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language Model
relevance: Finds causal emotion-related directions in Claude and links desperation interventions to unethical agent behavior.

title: HeadVis
authors: Robin Luger; Harish Kamath; Doug Finkbeiner et al.
relevance: Provides a head-centric workflow for sequence examples and optional feature-based QK and OV decomposition.

title: Natural Language Autoencoders Produce Unsupervised Explanations of LLM Activations

title: Downstream Connections Predict Which Features Will Steer Model Behavior

title: Turn-Averaged SAEs for Feature Discovery and Long-Context Attribution

title: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits

title: Transformer Feed-Forward Layers Are Key-Value Memories

title: Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT

title: Language Models Can Explain Neurons in Language Models

title: Eliciting Latent Predictions from Transformers with the Tuned Lens

title: Localizing Model Behavior with Path Patching

title: Improving Sparse Decomposition of Language Model Activations with Gated Sparse Autoencoders

title: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits

title: Sparse Feature Circuits: Discovering and Editing Interpretable Causal Graphs in Language Models

title: Information Flow Routes: Automatically Interpreting Language Models at Scale

title: Automatic Circuit Discovery with Language Models
relevance: Provides a unified edge-patching and mask-optimization library and demonstrates sensitivity to ablation choices.

title: InterpBench: Semi-Synthetic Transformers for Evaluating Mechanistic Interpretability Techniques

title: A Practical Review of Mechanistic Interpretability for Transformer-Based Language Models
authors: Daking Rai; Yilun Zhou; Shi Feng; Abulhair Saparov; Ziyu Yao

title: Mechanistic Interpretability for AI Safety: A Review

title: Gemma Scope: Open Sparse Autoencoders Everywhere All At Once on Gemma 2

title: Automatically Interpreting Millions of Features in Large Language Models

title: Jacobian Sparse Autoencoders: Sparsify Computations Not Just Activations

authors: Yinhan He; Wendy Zheng; Yushun Dong; Yaochen Zhu; Chen Chen; Jundong Li

title: Findings of the BlackboxNLP 2025 Shared Task: Localizing Circuits and Causal Variables in Language Models

title: Scalable Circuit Learning for Interpreting Large Language Models

title: Demystifying Variance in Circuit Discovery of LLMs

relevance: Introduces conductance-based attribution to reduce resampling variance while leaving prompt variance open.

authors: Ayan Antik Khan; Harsh Kohli; Yuekun Yao; Huan Sun; Ziyu Yao

relevance: Introduces contrast-consistent probing to recover latent binary knowledge without labeled truth data.

title: Steering Llama 2 via Contrastive Activation Addition

title: Transformer Circuits Thread

title: TransformerLens

title: NNsight

title: pyvene

title: Transformer Debugger

title: SAELens

title: dictionary_learning

title: circuit-tracer

title: AutoCircuit

title: MIB Circuit Track

title: ARENA

title: A Comprehensive Mechanistic Interpretability Explainer and Glossary
